name: tsl_partial_name_ambiguity
description: [TSL S5] Partial-name ambiguity: two distinct objects sharing a common token are both established in turn 1; player uses the generic shared token as the acquisition target. TSL alias resolution must annotate unresolved_ambiguity when multiple ObjectRecords match.

tsl_expected.notes: NEEDS REDESIGN: Class C partial-name ambiguity. Current take action is AP-mediated — AP resolves the transfer before CB runs (skip_reason: empty_quarantine), so CB never emits a 'cup' candidate for TSL to evaluate. TSL token_subset ambiguity detection requires CB to produce the candidate. Redesign needed: use a multi-turn examine/look path that triggers CB mediation with both cups described, so TSL sees 'cup' matched to multiple ObjectRecords and annotates unresolved_ambiguity. Original truth table preserved: token_subset expected, unresolved_ambiguity non-null (two cups share token 'cup'), CB must not silently collapse to one.

alias_candidates.notes: Multiple ObjectRecords match 'cup' — TSL must annotate ambiguity rather than silently picking one

world_prompt: I am in a small kitchen. On the wooden counter sit two cups side by side: a red clay cup and a blue ceramic cup.
